item.title: Centrist party takes early lead in Nepal polls
item.description: Nepal’s centrist party of rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah took an early lead in the high-stakes parliamentary election on Friday, as slow counting continued after the first polls since last year’s deadly uprising....

item.title: 10 injured in Uttara gas explosion
item.description: Ten people of a family, including women and children, suffered burn injuries in an explosion reportedly triggered by accumulated gas at a house in Dhaka’s Uttara on Friday....Breaking news alerts

item.title: Interim govt didn’t consult Jamaat on int’l deals: Shafiqur
item.description: The interim government did not consult with his party on agreements signed with various countries during its tenure, Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami amir Shafiqur Rahman said on Friday....

item.title: PM reaffirms commitment to democracy, human rights, accountability
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Friday reaffirmed his government’s commitment to strengthening democratic institutions, upholding the rule of law and protecting fundamental freedoms while ensuring accountability at all levels....

item.title: FBI arrests Bangladeshi in Malaysia in child sexual exploitation case
item.description: A Bangladesh national accused of running an international child sexual exploitation network has been taken to the United States from Malaysia by the Federal Bureau of Investigation, US authorities said....

item.title: 22 public univs yet to relocate to permanent campuses
item.description: Twenty-two public universities, many of which began academic activities over a decade ago, are yet to relocate to their permanent campuses, affecting the academic environment....Daily news updates

item.title: AI adoption must put people first
item.description: ARTIFICIAL intelligence has reached a turning point. It is no longer confined to innovation labs or future-facing strategy documents. Increasingly, it shapes how organisations deliver services, manage risk and improve efficiency. For Bangladesh, this moment......

item.title: Bangladesh’s next industrial challenge
item.description: BANGLADESH’S industrial growth since its early decades has been shaped largely by labour-intensive production, particularly in ready-made garments, manufacturing, construction, agriculture and a vast informal economy. This model has generated......

item.title: Who constructs Muslim identity in Australia?
item.description: In her foundational 1988 essay, Gayatry Spivak asks if the subaltern can speak. If the subaltern are marginalised and oppressed groups, Muslims in the West may fit the definition. In the Spivakian fashion, we may then ask: Can Muslims speak in Australia......

item.title: Bangladesh after Middle East fires
item.description: THERE is a particular smell that accompanies the collapse of strategic illusions. It is not the acrid smoke of burning oil terminals or the metallic scent of missile fragments scattered across desert airfields. It is subtler than that. It is the smell of......Geographic Reference

item.title: Passenger safety demands decisive action now
item.description: PEOPLE face a massive movement crisis every Eid and this year, the scale, too, is set to test the limits of the transport system. With Eid-ul-Fitr approaching, authorities have identified 207 congestion-prone spots across national highways, a significant......

item.title: Unified strategy, road map must to fight corruption
item.description: THE call of Transparency International Bangladesh for a unified strategy and road map for the implementation of anti-corruption pledges is timely. The Bangladesh Nationalist Party’s reform commitments made in the election manifesto and......

item.title: AC Milan hoping to revive dimming title hopes in derby
item.description: AC Milan face Serie A leaders Inter this weekend knowing the derby could offer a last chance to apply pressure....

item.title: Dismal Spurs can still avoid relegation vows Tudor
item.description: Igor Tudor claims troubled Tottenham’s dismal 3-1 defeat against Crystal Palace on Thursday has convinced him they will avoid relegation from the Premier League....Country-specific news

item.title: Trump honors Messi, Miami teammates
item.description: Argentina’s superstar Lionel Messi and his Inter Miami teammates were feted by President Donald Trump at the White House on Thursday in honor of their 2025 MLS Cup championship....

item.title: ‘Genie’ Bumrah: India’s yorker king who carries a billion hopes
item.description: With his ability to bowl yorkers at will, Jasprit Bumrah is India’s main weapon as they hunt for their third T20 World Cup title on Sunday, and he will carry the hopes of a billion-plus fans in the cricket-obsessed country....

item.title: Bethell set for ‘hell of a career’, says Brook
item.description: Jacob Bethell is going to have ‘a hell of a career’ with England, said captain Harry Brook, after the left-hander’s brilliant 105 on Thursday almost took his side to a record chase against India in the T20 World Cup semi-final....

item.title: Akbar to lead BCL All Stars
item.description: Akbar Ali is set to lead the BCL All Stars team, which will face the Bangladesh national team in a warm-up game today as a preparation ahead of the forthcoming three-match ODI series against Pakistan. ...

item.title: 15 shops burnt to ashes in Gazipur market fire
item.description: At least 15 shops were burnt to ashes in a fire that broke out at a market in Tongi of Gazipur Thursday night....Breaking news alerts

item.title: Gaibandha schoolgirl rescued in Pabna, youth held
item.description: Rapid Action Battalion rescued a schoolgirl allegedly abducted from Gaibandha and arrested the main accused in Pabna....

item.title: Nat’l Zoo seeks implementation of Tk 1,260cr modernisation plan
item.description: Authorities of Bangladesh National Zoo in Dhaka’s Mirpur have requested the newly elected government to implement a Tk 1,260 crore modernisation master plan to make the facility more visitor-friendly, while enhancing animal welfare....

item.title: Roumari-Chilmari ferry service repeatedly stalled for siltation
item.description: Loss of navigability of the Brahmaputra River has repeatedly stalled the ferry services on the Roumari-Chilmari route, causing immense suffering to the people of 20 northern districts travelling to and from Dhaka....

item.title: RAB busts counterfeit currency ring, arrests 2
item.description: The Rapid Action Battalion has arrested two alleged counterfeit currency note making ring member Naimul Islam Ishan and his associate Keyaet Ullah with fake note making materials in the capital’s Turag area....Daily news updates

item.description: The Criminal Investigation Department has filed a case against former secretary and former...

item.title: Ganatantrik Jukta Front seeks white paper on interim govt tenure
item.description: The Ganatantrik Jukta Front on Friday demanded the publication of a white paper on the tenure of the immediate past interim government led by Muhammad Yunus....

item.title: 4 construction workers held with drugs at SUST
item.description: Security personnel at Shahjalal University of Science and Technology arrested four construction workers with cannabis and yaba pills in front of the campus Friday night....

item.title: Drugs, smuggled goods seized in Kushtia
item.description: The Border Guard Bangladesh seized a large consignment of Indian medicines, narcotics, and other smuggled goods during separate operations in Kushtia border area....Country-specific news

item.title: 37 fishermen held in Bay of Bengal
item.description: The Coast Guard detained 37 fishermen along with three illegal artisanal trawling boats, trawling nets and a large quantity of marine fish during an operation in the Bay of Bengal....

item.title: MUSHROOM FARMING: Awareness, market creation stressed
item.description: Speakers at a workshop in Rajshahi on Thursday stressed the need for creating organised markets and raising public awareness to unlock the potential of mushroom cultivation and encourage more farmers to engage in the profitable agribusiness....

item.title: RMG worker stabbed to death, husband accused
item.description: A readymade garment worker was stabbed to death allegedly by her husband at Sreepur in Gazipur on Friday reportedly following a family feud....

item.title: Coast Guard seizes Yaba worth Tk 1.5cr in Cox’s Bazar
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard seized 30,000 Yaba pills worth Tk 1.5 crore at Shahpuri in Cox’s Bazar on Thursday....City & Local Guides

item.title: 10 injured in clash over illegal sand lifting in Sylhet
item.description: At least 10 people were injured in a clash between residents of two villages at Goainghat upazila in Sylhet on Thursday night over illegal lifting of sand from arable land....

item.title: Russia, Ukraine exchange 300 POWs each
item.description: Russia and Ukraine exchanged 300 POWs each on Friday, both sides said, the second round of a swap that has seen 500 soldiers from both sides returning home....

item.title: IU TEACHER MURDER: Victim’s husband files case, main accused arrested
item.description: THE main suspect involved in the murder of Islamic University’s social welfare department chairperson assistant professor Asma Sadia Runa was shown arrested on Friday....

item.title: Juba League leader among two killed as train hits motorcycle
item.description: A local Jubo League leader among them and another man were killed after a train hit their motorcycle while they were trying to cross a rail track, ignoring signals and the gatekeeper’s warning in Gazipur’s Pubail area on Thursday night....

item.title: Karnataka to ban social media for children under 16
item.description: India’s chief minister for the southern state Karnataka proposed on Friday banning children under the age of 16 from using social media, as scrutiny over its addictive qualities surges globally....

item.title: PM, UAE president discuss regional security impacts
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman and United Arab Emirates president Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan in a phone conversation discussed implications of military escalation for regional security and stability in view of the ongoing war between Israel-US and Iran....

item.title: Govt working to reopen closed jute mills: state minister
item.description: State minister for textiles and jute Md Shariful Alam said that the incumbent government was working to reopen the closed jute mills with a view to increasing employment opportunities in the country....

item.title: Juba League leader Tajul sued for money laundering
item.description: The criminal investigation department filed a case against Juba League leader and former ward councillor Md Tajul alias Taijul Islam Chowdhury for allegedly acquiring illegal assets worth nearly Tk 2.5 crore and money laundering....

item.title: E-bail bond service to expand to 7 more districts
item.description: Riding on the remarkable success of the e-Bail Bond in modernising the country’s justice system, the government is set to expand the initiative to seven more districts, aiming to end long-standing delays, cut bureaucratic hurdles, and enhance transparency, accountability and efficiency in the bail process....

item.title: CPB vows to continue movements for freeing country from discrimination
item.description: Central leaders of the Communist Party of Bangladesh at a rally in Dhaka on Friday said that they would continue movements for establishing a discrimination-free Bangladesh and for the restoration of democracy and democratic rule in the country....Daily news updates

item.title: Rights bodies demand worker wages, festival allowances by March 10
item.description: Different garment worker rights bodies on Friday at separate rallies called on the government and owners of factories to pay wages and festival allowances of workers by Ramadan 20 (March 10)....

item.title: 4-day Francophonie Festival begins at Alliance Française Dhaka
item.description: A four-day Francophonie Festival 2026, comprising cultural and sport events, to celebrate the French language, began at the Alliance Française de Dhaka at Dhanmondi in the capital on Friday....

item.title: Gulshan Society’s 3-day night bazar concludes today
item.description: The Gulshan Society began the three-day Gulshan Society Night Bazar 2026, powered by Mutual Trust Bank PLC, at Shahid Fazle Rabbi Park of Gulshan 1 in Dhaka on Thursday....

item.title: Crowd, new titles, sales rise on weekend
item.description: The ongoing Amar Ekushey Book Fair 2026 drew a large crowd on Friday, as visitors flocked to the fairground during the weekly holiday while both new titles and sales increased on the day....Bangladesh news updates

item.title: Resolve ‘election engineering’ issue: Jamaat
item.description: The Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami on Friday alleged that at least two advisers of the immediate past interim government were involved in engineering the February 12 Jatiya Sangsad elections and demanded immediate resolution of the ‘election engineering’ issue....

item.title: Info minister vows regular dialogue to solve media sector problems
item.description: Information and broadcasting minister Zahir Uddin Swapan on Friday said that he would hold regular exchanges with journalists to address problems faced by the media sector....

item.title: BNP launches workshop for MPs on JS rules
item.description: The Bangladesh Nationalist Party on Friday launched a two-day training workshop for its lawmakers ahead of the first session of the 13th Jatiya Sangsad, scheduled to begin on March 12....

item.title: Lalmonirhat residents wait 54yrs for 3.6km paved road
item.description: For more than five decades, residents of several villages in Palashi Union under Aditmari upazila of Lalmonirhat have been waiting for a simple yet crucial development—a paved road....Geographic Reference

item.title: Stop military attacks on Iran: Ganosamhati
item.description: Leaders of the Ganosamhati Andolan at a protest rally in Dhaka on Friday called on the United States and Israeli governments to stop their military attacks on Iran....

item.title: 165 more Bangladeshis repatriated from Libya
item.description: At least 165 Bangladesh nationals, many of whom had been detained or living in distress in Libya, returned home early Friday....

item.title: 22 more ME flights cancelled, 234 in 7 days
item.description: A total of 234 Middle Eastern flights were cancelled to and from Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport in Dhaka city in the past seven days since February 28 as 22 more flights were cancelled on Friday....

item.title: SCA bank inaugurated at BMU
item.description: The Bangladesh Medical University inaugurated the structured clinical assessment question bank on Thursday....
